themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe continue to gain traction in national polls, reflecting a broader trend of rising populist sentiment. This theme is evident in Germany, France, Italy, and Sweden, where parties like AfD, RN, and Fratelli d'Italia are either leading or making significant electoral gains.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads German polls with 27-28%, surpassing CDU/CSU and SPD.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella maintain strong polling ahead of the 2027 presidential election.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Fratelli d'Italia and Lega remain dominant in Italian polls, though recent data shows slight declines.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sweden Democrats gain influence in Swedish politics, shaping government policies.

themes.title: National priority policies spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The concept of 'national priority'—prioritizing citizens over immigrants in access to public services and benefits—has become a central policy debate in Spain, Italy, and other European countries. This theme highlights the legal and constitutional challenges posed by such policies, as well as their political implications.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox pushes 'national priority' policies in regional governments, sparking legal and political backlash.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP negotiates with Vox on 'national priority' policies, leading to internal divisions.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega and FdI debate similar policies, with Lega advocating for stricter immigration controls.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN promotes policies favoring French nationals in social services, aligning with broader European trends.

Vox avisa al PP que no permitirá que "diluya con retórica" la prioridad nacional firmada
Vox advierte al PP de que no permitirá que diluya la "prioridad nacional"

themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted assassination of former U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has dominated right-wing media coverage worldwide. This theme reflects the global reaction to the incident, including debates over security, political rhetoric, and media responsibility.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump and his allies blame political rhetoric and media for the shooting, demanding accountability.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK uses the incident to criticize media bias and political violence in the UK.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox links the shooting to broader critiques of political polarization and media irresponsibility.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN condemns political violence while using the incident to highlight security concerns.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: The resignation of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and the rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party have triggered a significant shift in Hungarian politics. This theme explores the implications of Orbán’s departure, including the redistribution of power, the future of Fidesz, and Hungary’s relations with the EU and Ukraine.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán resigns as PM, Fidesz undergoes leadership changes, and Tisza Party gains power.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS reacts to Orbán’s resignation, assessing its impact on regional alliances.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD comments on Orbán’s legacy and the future of Hungary’s right-wing politics.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU officials monitor Hungary’s political transition and its potential impact on EU-Hungary relations.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: The rise of far-right rhetoric in political discourse has been linked to an increase in political violence across multiple countries. This theme examines how inflammatory language and policies from far-right parties have contributed to a climate of hostility and violence, both online and offline.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega and FdI face criticism for rhetoric contributing to political tensions and violence.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s aggressive rhetoric sparks protests and accusations of inciting violence.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK’s policies and rhetoric are linked to rising tensions in local communities.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric is blamed for inspiring political violence, including assassination attempts.
